source.name: Bernie Sanders
source.notes_excerpt: Bernie Sanders is an American politician serving as the senior United States senator from Vermont, a seat he has held since 2007. He is the longest-serving independent in U.S. congressional history. Sanders was reelected to a fourth Senate term in 2024, defeating Republican Gerald Malloy. He currently serves as the Ranking Member of the Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee after

target.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
target.notes_excerpt: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) announced the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Data Center Moratorium Act on March 25, 2026, alongside Sen. Bernie Sanders. She announced on Friday that she is jumping into the race to be the top Democrat on the influential House Oversight Committee. source.name: Brian Deese
source.notes_excerpt: Brian Deese serves as Institute Innovation Fellow at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and Co-Founder and CEO at Foundry-Logic. He served as Director of the National Economic Council from 2021 to 2023 under President Biden. He previously worked as Global Head of Sustainable Investing at BlackRock and held various positions in the Obama administration including Senior Advisor to the President.

source.notes_excerpt: Chris Lehane is Chief Global Affairs Officer at OpenAI, having previously served as vice president of global policy starting in early 2024. He is a veteran of the Clinton administration who famously coined the term "vast right-wing conspiracy" and worked during the Monica Lewinsky scandal. target.name: Sam Altman
target.notes_excerpt: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the artificial intelligence research organization that developed ChatGPT. He became CEO of OpenAI in 2019 after co-founding the organization in 2015. In November 2023, he was briefly fired by OpenAI's board of directors but was reinstated five days later. His net worth was estimated at US$3.3 billion by Forbes in March 2026.
